findings.title: Russia launches massive daytime drone-missile assault on Ukraine
findings.summary: Russia conducted a historic daytime attack with over 550 drones and dozens of missiles, hitting hospitals, homes, and a UNESCO site, causing fatalities and injuries. This represents I2 military posture escalation beyond drone baseline due to scale and civilian targeting. Ukrainian forces report over 600 attacks in four days signaling spring offensive.
findings.claim_excerpt: Russia launched a historic daytime assault involving over 550 drones and dozens of missiles, striking hospitals, homes, and a UNESCO site
findings.preliminary_assessment: Signals intensified spring offensive, increasing pressure on Ukrainian defenses and civilian infrastructure.
findings.confidence_basis: Multiple cited outlets (NYT, AP) corroborate details.

sources_cited.excerpt: over 550 drones and dozens of missiles, striking hospitals
over six hundred attacks in the past four days

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: CFR aggregates Tier 1 sources; scale exceeds drone baseline.

findings.review_reason: Assess if part of broader spring offensive pattern

findings.title: Russia rejects Zelenskyy Easter ceasefire proposal
findings.summary: Zelenskyy proposed an Easter ceasefire, rejected by Russia, amid US-Ukraine talks on postwar security guarantees with NATO involvement. Affects I5 diplomatic channels, showing stalled de-escalation efforts. France-UK pledge troop deployments post-peace deal.
findings.claim_excerpt: Zelenskyy proposed an Easter ceasefire, which Russia rejected (Guardian)
findings.preliminary_assessment: Rejection maintains diplomatic impasse despite Western security pledges.
findings.confidence_basis: Corroborated by Guardian and Ukraine President statements.

sources_cited.excerpt: Kyiv and Washington agreed to fortify postwar guarantees

search_queries_run: Ukraine ceasefire proposal Russia reject April 2026
research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Tier 1 aggregation; aligns with ongoing patterns.

findings.title: U.S.-Israeli war with Iran enters third week
findings.summary: The U.S.-Israel war against Iran continues into its third week without end in sight. This I2 military posture escalation involves direct state-on-state conflict. Crisis Group provides 360-degree analysis of impacts.
findings.claim_excerpt: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran is entering its third week, with no end yet in sight
findings.preliminary_assessment: Prolonged direct warfare risks regional spillover.
findings.confidence_basis: Crisis Group Tier 1 report; Statista notes U.S.-Israel-Iran war.

sources_cited.excerpt: entering its third week, no end in sight

search_queries_run: Iran Israel US war update April 2026

findings.review_reason: Monitor for wider Middle East escalation

findings.title: Sudan fighting shifts to Kordofan, solidifying east-west divide
findings.summary: ACLED reports fighting moving to Kordofan as Sudan's east-west divide solidifies. This I2 military posture change expands conflict geography. Ongoing civil war causes collapsed systems and humanitarian crisis.
findings.claim_excerpt: Fighting moves to Kordofan as Sudan’s east–west divide solidifies
findings.preliminary_assessment: Geographic expansion hardens conflict lines, complicating aid.
findings.confidence_basis: ACLED watchlist and global trends report.

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Tier 1 systematic data; under-covered theatre signal.

findings.title: DRC eastern conflict at high escalation risk despite imminent Rwanda peace deal
findings.summary: DRC faces high escalation risk in east due to armed groups and weak governance, though Rwanda-DRC peace deal appears imminent. I2 posture driven by over 8,400 deaths in 2023; potential spread beyond east. Resource-rich but institutionally fragile.
findings.claim_excerpt: A peace deal between Rwanda and DRC appears imminent though potential for further escalation remains high
findings.preliminary_assessment: Peace deal may contain but risks persist from non-state actors.
findings.confidence_basis: GPI report; single primary but aligned with ACLED trends.

sources_cited.excerpt: potential for further escalation of conflict in eastern DRC

research_traceback.analyst_confidence_notes: Strong Tier 1 but needs fresh ACLED export for daily events.

findings.title: Ukraine war drives 6M+ refugees, 4.9M IDPs
findings.summary: Ongoing Russia-Ukraine war has caused over 6 million refugees and 4.9 million IDPs, creating Europe's new humanitarian emergency. I6 displacement velocity remains high amid intensified attacks. Figures from 2022 invasion escalation.
findings.claim_excerpt: over 6 million refugees... internal displacement of 4.9 million people
findings.preliminary_assessment: Persistent high displacement sustains humanitarian pressure.
findings.confidence_basis: WFP and CFR data align on scale.

sources_cited.excerpt: recent strikes force cities into shelters

below_threshold.title: Myanmar military capitalizes on foreign support ahead of elections
below_threshold.reason_excluded: No specific escalation indicator met; watchlist mention lacks daily event

theatre_summary.russia_ukraine: Intensified Russian assaults including record drone strikes signal spring offensive amid rejected ceasefire bids.
theatre_summary.gaza: Persistent high casualties and displacement with over 70,000 killed since 2023; fragile ceasefires noted regionally.
theatre_summary.sudan: Fighting shifts to Kordofan, solidifying east-west divide in ongoing civil war.
theatre_summary.myanmar: Military leverages foreign support ahead of elections; resistance divided.
theatre_summary.drc: Eastern conflict at high escalation risk despite potential Rwanda peace deal.
theatre_summary.iran: U.S.-Israeli war enters third week with no resolution.
